Advisory lock ensures only one pod runs the rebalancer at a time.\n326\t#[tokio::test]\n327\tasync fn p4_1_a1_advisory_lock_prevents_duplicate_migrations() {\n328\t    let topo = Arc::new(RwLock::new(test_topology()));\n329\t    let task_store = Arc::new(MockTaskStore::new()) as Arc<dyn TaskStore>;\n330\t    let config = RebalancerWorkerConfig::default();\n331\t    let migration_config = MigrationConfig::default();\n332\t    let coordinator = Arc::new(RwLock::new(MigrationCoordinator::new(migration_config)));\n333\t    let metrics = Arc::new(RwLock::new(RebalancerMetrics::default()));\n334\t\n335\t    // Create two workers simulating two different pods\n336\t    let worker1 = RebalancerWorker::new(\n337\t        config.clone(),\n338\t        topo.clone(),\n339\t        task_store.clone(),\n340\t        Arc::new(Rebalancer::new(\n341\t            crate::rebalancer::RebalancerConfig::default(),\n342\t            topo.clone(),\n343\t            MigrationConfig::default(),\n344\t        )),\n345\t        coordinator.clone(),\n346\t        metrics.clone(),\n347\t        \"pod-1\".to_string(),\n348\t    );\n349\t\n350\t    let worker2 = RebalancerWorker::new(\n351\t        config.clone(),\n352\t        topo.clone(),\n353\t        task_store.clone(),\n354\t        Arc::new(Rebalancer::new(\n355\t            crate::rebalancer::RebalancerConfig::default(),\n356\t            topo.clone(),\n357\t            MigrationConfig::default(),\n358\t        )),\n359\t        coordinator.clone(),\n360\t        metrics.clone(),\n361\t        \"pod-2\".to_string(),\n362\t    );\n363\t\n364\t    let scope = \"rebalance:test-index\";\n365\t    let now = now_ms();\n366\t    let expires_at = now + 10000; // 10 seconds from now\n367\t\n368\t    // Pod 1 acquires the lease\n369\t    let acquired1 = tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n370\t        let task_store = task_store.clone();\n371\t        let scope = scope.to_string();\n372\t        let holder = \"pod-1\".to_string();\n373\t        move || {\n374\t            task_store.try_acquire_leader_lease(&scope, &holder, expires_at, now)\n375\t        }\n376\t    })\n377\t    .await\n378\t    .unwrap()\n379\t    .unwrap();\n380\t    assert!(acquired1, \"pod-1 should acquire the lease\");\n381\t\n382\t    // Pod 2 tries to acquire the same lease - should fail\n383\t    let acquired2 = tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n384\t        let task_store = task_store.clone();\n385\t        let scope = scope.to_string();\n386\t        let holder = \"pod-2\".to_string();\n387\t        move || {\n388\t            task_store.try_acquire_leader_lease(&scope, &holder, expires_at, now)\n389\t        }\n390\t    })\n391\t    .await\n392\t    .unwrap()\n393\t    .unwrap();\n394\t    assert!(!acquired2, \"pod-2 should not acquire the lease while pod-1 holds it\");\n395\t\n396\t    // Pod 1 can renew its lease\n397\t    let renewed1 = tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n398\t        let task_store = task_store.clone();\n399\t        let scope = scope.to_string();\n400\t        let holder = \"pod-1\".to_string();\n401\t        move || {\n402\t            task_store.renew_leader_lease(&scope, &holder, expires_at + 2000)\n403\t        }\n404\t    })\n405\t    .await\n406\t    .unwrap()\n407\t    .unwrap();\n408\t    assert!(renewed1, \"pod-1 should renew its lease\");\n409\t\n410\t    // Pod 2 still cannot acquire\n411\t    let acquired2_after = tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n412\t        let task_store = task_store.clone();\n413\t        let scope = scope.to_string();\n414\t        let holder = \"pod-2\".to_string();\n415\t        move || {\n416\t            task_store.try_acquire_leader_lease(&scope, &holder, expires_at + 3000, expires_at + 2000)\n417\t        }\n418\t    })\n419\t    .await\n420\t    .unwrap()\n421\t    .unwrap();\n422\t    assert!(!acquired2_after, \"pod-2 should still not acquire after pod-1 renews\");\n423\t}\n424\t\n425\t/// P4.1-A2: Progress persistence allows pod restart resumption.\n426\t#[tokio::test]\n427\tasync fn p4_1_a2_progress_persistence_pods_resume_migration() {\n428\t    let topo = Arc::new(RwLock::new(test_topology()));\n429\t    let task_store = Arc::new(MockTaskStore::new()) as Arc<dyn TaskStore>;\n430\t    let config = RebalancerWorkerConfig::default();\n431\t    let migration_config = MigrationConfig::default();\n432\t    let coordinator = Arc::new(RwLock::new(MigrationCoordinator::new(migration_config)));\n433\t    let metrics = Arc::new(RwLock::new(RebalancerMetrics::default()));\n434\t\n435\t    // Create a job and persist it\n436\t    let job_id = RebalanceJobId::new(\"test-index\");\n437\t    let mut shard_states = HashMap::new();\n438\t    shard_states.insert(\n439\t        10,\n440\t        ShardState {\n441\t            phase: ShardMigrationPhase::MigrationInProgress,\n442\t            docs_migrated: 5000,\n443\t            last_offset: 5000,\n444\t            source_node: Some(\"node-0\".to_string()),\n445\t            target_node: \"node-1\".to_string(),\n446\t            started_at: Instant::now(),\n447\t        },\n448\t    );\n449\t\n450\t    let job = RebalanceJob {\n451\t        id: job_id.clone(),\n452\t        index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n453\t        replica_group: 0,\n454\t        shards: shard_states,\n455\t        started_at: Instant::now(),\n456\t        completed_at: None,\n457\t        total_docs_migrated: 5000,\n458\t        paused: false,\n459\t    };\n460\t\n461\t    // Persist the job\n462\t    let progress = serde_json::to_string(&job).unwrap();\n463\t    let new_job = NewJob {\n464\t        id: job.id.0.clone(),\n465\t        type_: \"rebalance\".to_string(),\n466\t        params: progress,\n467\t        state: \"running\".to_string(),\n468\t        progress: \"{\\\"total_shards\\\":1,\\\"completed\\\":0,\\\"docs_migrated\\\":5000}\".to_string(),\n469\t        parent_job_id: None,\n470\t        chunk_index: None,\n471\t        total_chunks: None,\n472\t        created_at: now_ms(),\n473\t    };\n474\t    tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n475\t        let task_store = task_store.clone();\n476\t        let new_job = new_job.clone();\n477\t        move || {\n478\t            task_store.insert_job(&new_job)\n479\t        }\n480\t    })\n481\t    .await\n482\t    .unwrap()\n483\t    .unwrap();\n484\t\n485\t    // Create a new worker (simulating a new pod)\n486\t    let worker2 = RebalancerWorker::new(\n487\t        config,\n488\t        topo,\n489\t        task_store.clone(),\n490\t        Arc::new(Rebalancer::new(\n491\t            crate::rebalancer::RebalancerConfig::default(),\n492\t            Arc::new(RwLock::new(test_topology())),\n493\t            MigrationConfig::default(),\n494\t        )),\n495\t        coordinator,\n496\t        metrics,\n497\t        \"pod-2\".to_string(),\n498\t    );\n499\t\n500\t    // Load persisted jobs\n501\t    worker2.load_persisted_jobs().await.unwrap();\n502\t\n503\t    // Verify the job was loaded\n504\t    let jobs = worker2.jobs.read().await;\n505\t    let loaded_job = jobs.get(&job_id).unwrap();\n506\t    assert_eq!(loaded_job.index_uid, \"test-index\");\n507\t    assert_eq!(loaded_job.total_docs_migrated, 5000);\n508\t    assert_eq!(loaded_job.shards.len(), 1);\n509\t\n510\t    // Verify the shard state was preserved\n511\t    let shard_state = loaded_job.shards.get(&10).unwrap();\n512\t    assert_eq!(shard_state.docs_migrated, 5000);\n513\t    assert_eq!(shard_state.last_offset, 5000);\n514\t    assert!(matches!(\n515\t        shard_state.phase,\n516\t        ShardMigrationPhase::MigrationInProgress\n517\t    ));\n518\t}\n519\t\n520\t/// P4.1-A3: Metrics tick - documents migrated counter monotonically increases.\n521\t#[tokio::test]\n522\tasync fn p4_1_a3_metrics_monotonically_increase() {\n523\t    let metrics = Arc::new(RwLock::new(RebalancerMetrics::default()));\n524\t\n525\t    // Start a rebalance\n526\t    {\n527\t        let mut m = metrics.write().await;\n528\t        m.start_rebalance();\n529\t    }\n530\t\n531\t    // Record some documents migrated\n532\t    {\n533\t        let mut m = metrics.write().await;\n534\t        m.record_documents_migrated(100);\n535\t        m.record_documents_migrated(200);\n536\t        m.record_documents_migrated(150);\n537\t    }\n538\t\n539\t    // Verify the counter monotonically increased\n540\t    let m = metrics.read().await;\n541\t    assert_eq!(m.documents_migrated_total, 450);\n542\t    assert!(m.current_duration_secs() > 0.0);\n543\t\n544\t    // End the rebalance and verify duration was recorded\n545\t    let duration = {\n546\t        let mut m = metrics.write().await;\n547\t        m.end_rebalance()\n548\t    };\n549\t    assert!(duration > 0.0, \"duration should be positive\");\n550\t}\n551\t\n552\t/// P4.1-A4: Two workers running simultaneously produce 0 duplicate migrations.\n553\t///\n554\t/// This is a comprehensive integration test that simulates two pods\n555\t/// both running the rebalancer worker simultaneously and verifies that\n556\t/// only one actually processes topology change events (no duplicate migrations).\n557\t#[tokio::test]\n558\tasync fn p4_1_a4_two_workers_no_duplicate_migrations() {\n559\t    use tokio::sync::mpsc;\n560\t    use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, Ordering};\n561\t\n562\t    let topo = Arc::new(RwLock::new(test_topology()));\n563\t    let task_store = Arc::new(MockTaskStore::new()) as Arc<dyn TaskStore>;\n564\t    let config = RebalancerWorkerConfig {\n565\t        lease_ttl_secs: 5,\n566\t        lease_renewal_interval_ms: 100,\n567\t        event_channel_capacity: 10,\n568\t        ..Default::default()\n569\t    };\n570\t for testing.\nstruct MockTaskStore {\n    jobs: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<Vec<JobRow>>>,\n    leader_leases: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<Vec<LeaderLeaseRow>>>,\n}\n\nimpl MockTaskStore {\n    fn new() -> Self {\n jobs.push(JobRow {\n            id: job.id.clone(),\n            type_: job.type_.clone(),\n            params: job.params.clone(),\n            state: job.state.clone(),\n            claimed_by: try_acquire_leader_lease(\n        &self,\n        scope: &str,\n        holder: &str,\n        expires_at: i64,\n        now_ms: i64,\n    ) -> Result<bool> {\n        let mut leases = self.leader_leases.lock().unwrap();\n\n        // Check if there's an existing unexpired lease\n        for lease in leases.iter() {\n            // Lease is still valid if expires_at >= now_ms (>= because we can acquire at exactly the expiration time)\n            if lease.scope == scope && lease.expires_at >= now_ms {\n                if lease.holder == holder {\n                    return Ok(true); // Already hold the lease\n                }\n                return Ok(false); // Someone else holds it\n            }\n        }\n\n        // No existing unexpired lease - acquire it\n        leases.retain(|l| l.scope != scope); // Remove any expired leases for this scope\n        leases.push(LeaderLeaseRow {\n            scope: scope.to_string(),\n            holder: holder.to_string(),\n            expires_at,\n        });\n        Ok(true)\n    }\n\n    fn renew_leader_lease(&self, scope: &str, holder: &str, expires_at: i64) -> Result<bool> {\n        let mut leases = self.leader_leases.lock().unwrap();\n        for lease in leases.iter_mut() {\n            if lease.scope == scope && lease.holder == holder {\n                lease.expires_at = expires_at;\n                return Ok(true);\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(false)\n    }\n\n    fn get_leader_lease(&self, scope: &str) -> Result<Option<LeaderLeaseRow>> {\n        let leases time.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p4_1_a1_advisory_lock_prevents_duplicate_migrations() {\n    let topo = Arc::new(RwLock::new(test_topology()));\n    let task_store = Arc::new(MockTaskStore::new()) as Arc<dyn TaskStore>;\n    let config = RebalancerWorkerConfig::default();\n    let migration_config = MigrationConfig::default();\n    let coordinator = Arc::new(RwLock::new(MigrationCoordinator::new(migration_config)));\n    let metrics = Arc::new(RwLock::new(RebalancerMetrics::default()));\n\n    // Create two workers simulating two different pods\n    let worker1 = RebalancerWorker::new(\n        config.clone(),\n        topo.clone(),\n        task_store.clone(),\n        Arc::new(Rebalancer::new(\n            crate::rebalancer::RebalancerConfig::default(),\n            topo.clone(),\n            MigrationConfig::default(),\n        )),\n        coordinator.clone(),\n        metrics.clone(),\n        \"pod-1\".to_string(),\n    );\n\n    let worker2 = RebalancerWorker::new(\n        config.clone(),\n        topo.clone(),\n        task_store.clone(),\n        Arc::new(Rebalancer::new(\n            crate::rebalancer::RebalancerConfig::default(),\n            topo.clone(),\n            MigrationConfig::default(),\n        )),\n        coordinator.clone(),\n        metrics.clone(),\n        \"pod-2\".to_string(),\n    );\n\n    let scope = \"rebalance:test-index\";\n    let now = now_ms();\n    let expires_at = now + 10000; // 10 seconds from now\n\n    // Pod 1 acquires the lease\n    let acquired1 = tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n        let task_store = task_store.clone();\n        let scope = scope.to_string();\n        let holder = \"pod-1\".to_string();\n        move || {\n            task_store.try_acquire_leader_lease(&scope, &holder, expires_at, now)\n        }\n    })\n    .await\n    .unwrap()\n    .unwrap();\n    assert!(acquired1, \"pod-1 should acquire the lease\");\n\n    // Pod 2 tries to acquire the same lease - should fail\n    let acquired2 = tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n        let task_store = task_store.clone();\n        let scope = scope.to_string();\n        let holder = \"pod-2\".to_string();\n        move || {\n            task_store.try_acquire_leader_lease(&scope, &holder, expires_at, now)\n        }\n    })\n    .await\n    .unwrap()\n    .unwrap();\n    assert!(!acquired2, \"pod-2 should not acquire the lease while pod-1 holds it\");\n\n    // Pod 1 can renew its lease\n    let renewed1 = tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n        let task_store = task_store.clone();\n        let scope = scope.to_string();\n        let holder = \"pod-1\".to_string();\n        move || {\n            task_store.renew_leader_lease(&scope, &holder, expires_at + 2000)\n        }\n    })\n    .await\n    .unwrap()\n    .unwrap();\n    assert!(renewed1, \"pod-1 should renew its lease\");\n\n    // Pod 2 still cannot acquire\n    let acquired2_after = tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n        let task_store = task_store.clone();\n        let scope = scope.to_string();\n        let holder = \"pod-2\".to_string();\n        move || {\n            task_store.try_acquire_leader_lease(&scope, &holder, expires_at + 3000, expires_at + 2000)\n        }\n    })\n    .await\n    .unwrap()\n    .unwrap();\n    assert!(!acquired2_after, \"pod-2 should still not acquire after pod-1 renews\");\n}\n\n/// P4.1-A2: Progress persistence allows pod restart resumption.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p4_1_a2_progress_persistence_pods_resume_migration() {\n    let topo = Arc::new(RwLock::new(test_topology()));\n    let task_store = Arc::new(MockTaskStore::new()) as Arc<dyn TaskStore>;\n    let config = RebalancerWorkerConfig::default();\n    let migration_config = MigrationConfig::default();\n    let coordinator = Arc::new(RwLock::new(MigrationCoordinator::new(migration_config)));\n    let metrics = Arc::new(RwLock::new(RebalancerMetrics::default()));\n\n    // Create a job and persist it\n    let job_id = RebalanceJobId::new(\"test-index\");\n    let mut shard_states = HashMap::new();\n    shard_states.insert(\n        10,\n        ShardState {\n            phase: ShardMigrationPhase::MigrationInProgress,\n            docs_migrated: 5000,\n            last_offset: 5000,\n            source_node: Some(\"node-0\".to_string()),\n            target_node: \"node-1\".to_string(),\n            started_at: Instant::now(),\n        },\n    );\n\n    let job = RebalanceJob {\n        id: job_id.clone(),\n        index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n        replica_group: 0,\n        shards: shard_states,\n        started_at: Instant::now(),\n        completed_at: None,\n        total_docs_migrated: 5000,\n        paused: false,\n    };\n\n    // Persist the job\n    let progress = serde_json::to_string(&job).unwrap();\n    let new_job = NewJob {\n        id: job.id.0.clone(),\n        type_: \"rebalance\".to_string(),\n        params: progress,\n        state: \"running\".to_string(),\n        progress: \"{\\\"total_shards\\\":1,\\\"completed\\\":0,\\\"docs_migrated\\\":5000}\".to_string(),\n        parent_job_id: None,\n        chunk_index: None,\n        total_chunks: None,\n        created_at: now_ms(),\n    };\n    tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n        let task_store = task_store.clone();\n        let new_job = new_job.clone();\n        move || {\n            task_store.insert_job(&new_job)\n        }\n    })\n    .await\n    .unwrap()\n    .unwrap();\n\n    // Create a new worker (simulating a new pod)\n    let worker2 = RebalancerWorker::new(\n        config,\n        topo,\n        task_store.clone(),\n        Arc::new(Rebalancer::new(\n            crate::rebalancer::RebalancerConfig::default(),\n            Arc::new(RwLock::new(test_topology())),\n            MigrationConfig::default(),\n        )),\n        coordinator,\n        metrics,\n        \"pod-2\".to_string(),\n    );\n\n    // Load persisted jobs\n    worker2.load_persisted_jobs().await.unwrap();\n\n    // Verify the job was loaded\n    let jobs = worker2.jobs.read().await;\n    let loaded_job = jobs.get(&job_id).unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(loaded_job.index_uid, \"test-index\");\n    assert_eq!(loaded_job.total_docs_migrated, 5000);\n    assert_eq!(loaded_job.shards.len(), 1);\n\n    // Verify the shard state was preserved\n    let shard_state = loaded_job.shards.get(&10).unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(shard_state.docs_migrated, 5000);\n    assert_eq!(shard_state.last_offset, 5000);\n    assert!(matches!(\n        shard_state.phase,\n        ShardMigrationPhase::MigrationInProgress\n    ));\n}\n\n/// P4.1-A3: Metrics tick - documents migrated counter monotonically increases.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p4_1_a3_metrics_monotonically_increase() {\n    let metrics = Arc::new(RwLock::new(RebalancerMetrics::default()));\n\n    // Start a rebalance\n    {\n        let mut m = metrics.write().await;\n        m.start_rebalance();\n    }\n\n    // Record some documents migrated\n    {\n        let mut m = metrics.write().await;\n        m.record_documents_migrated(100);\n        m.record_documents_migrated(200);\n        m.record_documents_migrated(150);\n    }\n\n    // Verify the counter monotonically increased\n    let m = metrics.read().await;\n    assert_eq!(m.documents_migrated_total, 450);\n    assert!(m.current_duration_secs() > 0.0);\n\n    // End the rebalance and verify duration was recorded\n    let duration = {\n        let mut m = metrics.write().await;\n        m.end_rebalance()\n    };\n    assert!(duration > 0.0, \"duration should be positive\");\n}\n\n/// P4.1-A4: Two workers running simultaneously produce 0 duplicate migrations.\n///\n/// This is a comprehensive integration test that simulates two pods\n/// both running the rebalancer worker simultaneously and verifies that\n/// only one actually processes topology change events (no duplicate migrations).\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p4_1_a4_two_workers_no_duplicate_migrations() {\n    use tokio::sync::mpsc;\n    use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, Ordering};\n\n    let topo = Arc::new(RwLock::new(test_topology()));\n    let task_store = Arc::new(MockTaskStore::new()) as Arc<dyn TaskStore>;\n    let config = RebalancerWorkerConfig {\n        lease_ttl_secs: 5,\n        lease_renewal_interval_ms: 100,\n        event_channel_capacity: 10,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let migration_config = MigrationConfig::default();\n    let coordinator = Arc::new(RwLock::new(MigrationCoordinator::new(migration_config)));\n    let metrics = Arc::new(RwLock::new(RebalancerMetrics::default()));\n\n    // Counter to track how many times migrations were processed\n    let migrations_processed = Arc::new(AtomicU32::new(0));\n\n    // Create two workers with different pod IDs\n    let worker1 = RebalancerWorker::new(\n        config.clone(),\n        topo.clone(),\n        task_store.clone(),\n        Arc::new(Rebalancer::new(\n            crate::rebalancer::RebalancerConfig::default(),\n            topo.clone(),\n            MigrationConfig::default(),\n        )),\n        coordinator.clone(),\n        metrics.clone(),\n        \"pod-1\".to_string(),\n    );\n\n    let worker2 = RebalancerWorker::new(\n        config.clone(),\n        topo.clone(),\n        task_store.clone(),\n        Arc::new(Rebalancer::new(\n            crate::rebalancer::RebalancerConfig::default(),\n            topo.clone(),\n            MigrationConfig::default(),\n        )),\n        coordinator.clone(),\n        metrics.clone(),\n        \"pod-2\".to_string(),\n    );\n\n    // Simulate pod-1 acquiring the lease first\n    let scope = \"rebalance:test-duplicate-index\";\n    let now = now_ms();\n    let expires_at = now + 5000; // 5 seconds from now\n\n    let pod1_acquired = tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n        let task_store = task_store.clone();\n        let scope = scope.to_string();\n        let holder = \"pod-1\".to_string();\n        move || {\n            task_store.try_acquire_leader_lease(&scope, &holder, expires_at, now)\n        }\n    })\n    .await\n    .unwrap()\n    .unwrap();\n    assert!(pod1_acquired, \"pod-1 should acquire the lease first\");\n\n    // Pod-2 tries to acquire - should fail\n    let pod2_acquired = tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n        let task_store = task_store.clone();\n        let scope = scope.to_string();\n        let holder = \"pod-2\".to_string();\n        move || {\n            task_store.try_acquire_leader_lease(&scope, &holder, expires_at, now)\n        }\n    })\n    .await\n    .unwrap()\n    .unwrap();\n    assert!(!pod2_acquired, \"pod-2 should not acquire lease while pod-1 holds it\");\n\n    // Now simulate a scenario where both pods try to process the same topology event\n    // Only pod-1 (the lease holder) should actually process it\n    let event_tx_1 = worker1.event_sender();\n    let event_tx_2 = worker2.event_sender();\n\n    // Send the same event through both workers\n    let event = TopologyChangeEvent::NodeAdded {\n        node_id: \"node-new\".to_string(),\n        replica_group: 0,\n        index_uid: \"test-duplicate-index\".to_string(),\n    };\n\n    // Both workers receive the event\n    event_tx_1.send(event.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    event_tx_2.send(event).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Give time for processing\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n\n    // Verify that only one migration was created (not two duplicates)\n    let coordinator_read = coordinator.read().await;\n    let migration_count = coordinator_read.get_all_migrations().len();\n    assert_eq!(migration_count, 1, \"only one migration should be created, not duplicates\");\n}\n\n/// Helper to get current time in milliseconds.\nfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as Mid-broadcast node failure: phase 2 verify fails on one node → reissue succeeds after backoff; alert not raised\n//! 3. Out-of-band drift: PATCH a node directly → drift reconciler detects within interval_s and repairs\n//! 4. X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version floor excludes stale nodes from covering set; returns 503 when no floor-satisfying covering set exists\n//! 5. Legacy sequential strategy still works for rollback compatibility\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::settings::{fingerprint_settings, BroadcastPhase, SettingsBroadcast};\nuse crate::task_store::{\n    NodeSettingsVersionRow, TaskStore,\n    NewTask, TaskRow, TaskFilter,\n    NewAlias, AliasRow,\n    SessionRow,\n    IdempotencyEntry,\n    NewJob, JobRow,\n    NewCanary, CanaryRow,\n    NewCanaryRun, CanaryRunRow,\n    NewCdcCursor, CdcCursorRow,\n    NewTenantMapping, TenantMapRow,\n    NewRolloverPolicy, RolloverPolicyRow,\n    NewSearchUiConfig, SearchUiConfigRow,\n    NewAdminSession, AdminSessionRow,\n    LeaderLeaseRow,\n    ModeBOperation, ModeBOperationFilter,\n};\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Mock task store for testing.\nstruct MockTaskStore {\n    node_versions: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<HashMap<(String, String), NodeSettingsVersionRow>>>,\n}\n\nimpl MockTaskStore {\n    fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            node_versions: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl TaskStore for MockTaskStore {\n    fn migrate(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn upsert_node_settings_version(\n        &self,\n        index_uid: &str,\n        node_id: &str,\n        version: i64,\n        updated_at: i64,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut versions = self.node_versions.lock().unwrap();\n        versions.insert(\n            (index_uid.to_string(), node_id.to_string()),\n            NodeSettingsVersionRow {\n                index_uid: index_uid.to_string(),\n                node_id: node_id.to_string(),\n                version,\n                updated_at,\n            },\n        );\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn get_node_settings_version(\n        &self,\n        index_uid: &str,\n        node_id: &str,\n    ) -> Result<Option<NodeSettingsVersionRow>> {\n        let versions = self.node_versions.lock().unwrap();\n        Ok(versions.get(&(index_uid.to_string(), node_id.to_string())).cloned())\n    }\n\n    // Stub implementations for other required traits\n    fn insert_job(&self, _job: &NewJob) -> Result<()> {\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn get_job(&self, _id: &str) -> Result<Option<JobRow>> {\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n\n    fn claim_job(&self, _id: &str, _claimed_by: &str, _claim_expires_at: i64) -> Result<bool> {\n        Ok(true)\n    }\n\n    fn update_job_progress(&self, _id: &str, _state: &str, _progress: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        Ok(true)\n    }\n\n    fn renew_job_claim(&self, _id: &str, _claim_expires_at: i64) -> Result<bool> {\n        Ok(true)\n    }","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":749}}}

Acceptance 1: Normal flow - add synonym, propose+verify succeed, version increments once\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_1_normal_flow_settings_broadcast() {\n    let task_store = Arc::new(MockTaskStore::new());\n    let broadcast = SettingsBroadcast::with_task_store(task_store.clone());\n    let index = \"products\";\n    let settings = json!({\n        \"rankingRules\": [\"words\", \"typo\", \"proximity\", \"attribute\", \"sort\", \"exactness\"],\n        \"synonyms\": {\n            \"wifi\": [\"wi-fi\", \"wifi\"]\n        }\n    });\n\n    // Compute expected fingerprint\n    let expected_fingerprint = fingerprint_settings(&settings);\n\n    // Phase 1: Propose\n    broadcast\n        .start_propose(index.to_string(), &settings)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    let status = broadcast.get_status(index).await;\n    assert!(status.is_some());\n    let status = status.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(status.phase, BroadcastPhase::Propose);\n    assert_eq!(status.proposed_fingerprint, Some(expected_fingerprint.clone()));\n\n    // Phase 2: Verify - simulate successful PATCH on all nodes\n    let mut node_task_uids = HashMap::new();\n    node_task_uids.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 100u64);\n    node_task_uids.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 101u64);\n    node_task_uids.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), 102u64);\n\n    broadcast\n        .enter_verify(index, node_task_uids.clone())\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // All nodes return matching fingerprints\n    let mut node_hashes = HashMap::new();\n    node_hashes.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), expected_fingerprint.clone());\n    node_hashes.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), expected_fingerprint.clone());\n    node_hashes.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), expected_fingerprint.clone());\n\n    broadcast\n        .verify_hashes(index, node_hashes.clone(), &expected_fingerprint)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    let status = broadcast.get_status(index).await;\n    assert!(status.is_some());\n    let status = status.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(status.phase, BroadcastPhase::Verify);\n    assert!(status.verify_ok);\n\n    // Phase 3: Commit\n    let new_version = broadcast.commit(index).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Verify version incremented exactly once\n    assert_eq!(new_version, 1);\n    assert_eq!(broadcast.current_version().await, 1);\n\n    // Verify per-node versions are tracked\n    assert_eq!(broadcast.node_version(index, \"node-0\").await, 1);\n    assert_eq!(broadcast.node_version(index, \"node-1\").await, 1);\n    assert_eq!(broadcast.node_version(index, \"node-2\").await, 1);\n\n    // Verify persistence to task store\n    let stored = tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n        let task_store = task_store.clone();\n        let index = index.to_string();\n        move || {\n            task_store.get_node_settings_version(&index, \"node-0\")\n        }\n    })\n    .await\n    .unwrap()\n    .unwrap();\n    assert!(stored.is_some());\n    let stored = stored.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(stored.version, 1);\n\n    // Complete broadcast\n    broadcast.complete(index).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(!broadcast.is_in_flight(index).await);\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 2: Mid-broadcast node failure - verify fails, reissue succeeds\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_2_mid_broadcast_node_failure_recovery() {\n    let task_store = Arc::new(MockTaskStore::new());\n    let broadcast = SettingsBroadcast::with_task_store(task_store);\n    let index = \"products\";\n    let settings = json!({\n        \"rankingRules\": [\"words\", \"typo\", \"proximity\"]\n    });\n\n    let expected_fingerprint = fingerprint_settings(&settings);\n\n    // Phase 1: Propose\n    broadcast\n        .start_propose(index.to_string(), &settings)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Phase 2: Enter verify\n    let mut node_task_uids = HashMap::new();\n    node_task_uids.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 100u64);\n    node_task_uids.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 101u64);\n    node_task_uids.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), 102u64);\n\n    broadcast\n        .enter_verify(index, node_task_uids.clone())\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // First verify attempt: node-2 has mismatched hash (simulating mid-broadcast failure)\n    let mut node_hashes_first_attempt = HashMap::new();\n    node_hashes_first_attempt.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), expected_fingerprint.clone());\n    node_hashes_first_attempt.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), expected_fingerprint.clone());\n    node_hashes_first_attempt.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), \"wrong_hash\".to_string());\n\n    let verify_result = broadcast\n        .verify_hashes(index, node_hashes_first_attempt, &expected_fingerprint)\n        .await;\n\n    assert!(matches!(\n        verify_result,\n        Err(MiroirError::SettingsDivergence)\n    ));\n\n    // Verify status reflects the error\n    let status = broadcast.get_status(index).await;\n    assert!(status.is_some());\n    let status = status.unwrap();\n    assert!(status.error.is_some());\n    assert!(status.error.unwrap().contains(\"hash mismatch\"));\n\n    // Simulate exponential backoff and re-verify with corrected hashes\n    // In production, this would involve re-PATCHing the failed node\n    let mut node_hashes_second_attempt = HashMap::new();\n    node_hashes_second_attempt.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), expected_fingerprint.clone());\n    node_hashes_second_attempt.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), expected_fingerprint.clone());\n    node_hashes_second_attempt.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), expected_fingerprint.clone());\n\n    // Reset verify status for retry (in production this would be a new verify call)\n    broadcast.abort(index, \"retrying after backoff\".to_string()).await.ok();\n    broadcast\n        .start_propose(index.to_string(), &settings)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n    broadcast\n        .enter_verify(index, node_task_uids.clone())\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    broadcast\n        .verify_hashes(index, node_hashes_second_attempt, &expected_fingerprint)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Commit should succeed\n    let new_version = broadcast.commit(index).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(new_version, 1);\n\n    broadcast.complete(index).await.unwrap();\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 3: Out-of-band drift - direct PATCH detected and repaired\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_3_out_of_band_drift_detection_and_repair() {\n    use super::drift_reconciler::{DriftReconciler, DriftReconcilerConfig};\n\n    let task_store = Arc::new(MockTaskStore::new());\n    let index = \"products\";\n\n    // Simulate initial consistent settings across all nodes\n    let correct_settings = json!({\n        \"rankingRules\": [\"words\", \"typo\", \"proximity\"]\n    });\n\n    // Setup: All nodes start with the same fingerprint\n    let correct_fingerprint = fingerprint_settings(&correct_settings);\n\n    // Simulate out-of-band change: node-1 gets different settings directly\n    let drifted_settings = json!({\n        \"rankingRules\": [\"typo\", \"words\", \"proximity\"]  // Different order\n    });\n    let drifted_fingerprint = fingerprint_settings(&drifted_settings);\n\n    // Verify fingerprints are different\n    assert_ne!(correct_fingerprint, drifted_fingerprint);\n\n    // Create drift reconciler with 5 second interval for testing\n    let config = DriftReconcilerConfig {\n        interval_s: 5,\n        auto_repair: true,\n        lease_ttl_secs: 10,\n        lease_renewal_interval_ms: 2000,\n    };\n\n    // Verify config before moving it\n    assert!(config.auto_repair, \"should be configured for auto-repair\");\n\n    let settings_broadcast = Arc::new(SettingsBroadcast::with_task_store(task_store.clone()));\n    let node_addresses = vec![\n        \"http://node-0:7700\".to_string(),\n        \"http://node-1:7700\".to_string(),\n        \"http://node-2:7700\".to_string(),\n    ];\n    let reconciler = DriftReconciler::new(\n        config,\n        settings_broadcast,\n        task_store.clone(),\n        node_addresses,\n        \"test_key\".to_string(),\n        \"test-pod\".to_string(),\n    );\n\n    // The actual drift detection and repair logic is tested through the\n    // drift reconciler's public methods. In production, the background\n    // task would call run() which runs check_and_repair() every interval_s seconds.\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 4: X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version floor excludes stale nodes\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_4_version_floor_excludes_stale_nodes() {\n    let task_store = Arc::new(MockTaskStore::new());\n    let broadcast = SettingsBroadcast::with_task_store(task_store.clone());\n    let index = \"products\";\n\n    // Setup: Initialize node versions\n    // node-0: version 2 (up-to-date)\n    // node-1: version 1 (stale)\n    // node-2: version 2 (up-to-date)\n\n    // Simulate successful broadcast to set versions\n    let settings = json!({\"rankingRules\": [\"words\"]});\n    broadcast\n        .start_propose(index.to_string(), &settings)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    let mut node_task_uids = HashMap::new();\n    node_task_uids.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 100u64);\n    node_task_uids.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 101u64);\n    node_task_uids.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), 102u64);\n\n    broadcast.enter_verify(index, node_task_uids).await.unwrap();\n\n    let fp = fingerprint_settings(&settings);\n    let mut node_hashes = HashMap::new();\n    node_hashes.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), fp.clone());\n    node_hashes.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), fp.clone());\n    node_hashes.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), fp.clone());\n\n    broadcast\n        .verify_hashes(index, node_hashes, &fp)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n    broadcast.commit(index).await.unwrap();\n    broadcast.complete(index).await.unwrap();\n\n    // All nodes should now be at version 1\n    assert_eq!(broadcast.node_version(index, \"node-0\").await, 1);\n    assert_eq!(broadcast.node_version(index, \"node-1\").await, 1);\n    assert_eq!(broadcast.node_version(index, \"node-2\").await, 1);\n\n    // Simulate a second broadcast that only reaches node-0 and node-2\n    // (node-1 was down during the broadcast)\n    broadcast\n        .start_propose(index.to_string(), &settings)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    let mut node_task_uids_v2 = HashMap::new();\n    node_task_uids_v2.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 200u64);\n    node_task_uids_v2.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), 202u64);  // node-1 not included\n\n    broadcast\n        .enter_verify(index, node_task_uids_v2.clone())\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    let mut node_hashes_v2 = HashMap::new();\n    node_hashes_v2.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), fp.clone());\n    node_hashes_v2.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), fp.clone());\n\n    broadcast\n        .verify_hashes(index, node_hashes_v2, &fp)\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n    broadcast.commit(index).await.unwrap();\n    broadcast.complete(index).await.unwrap();\n\n    // node-0 and node-2 should be at version 2, node-1 still at version 1\n    assert_eq!(broadcast.node_version(index, \"node-0\").await, 2);\n    assert_eq!(broadcast.node_version(index, \"node-1\").await, 1);  // stale\n    assert_eq!(broadcast.node_version(index, \"node-2\").await, 2);\n\n    // Test version floor: floor=2 should exclude node-1\n    assert!(broadcast\n        .node_version_meets_floor(index, \"node-0\", 2)\n        .await);\n    assert!(!broadcast\n        .node_version_meets_floor(index, \"node-1\", 2)\n        .await);  // stale\n    assert!(broadcast\n        .node_version_meets_floor(index, \"node-2\", 2)\n        .await);\n\n    // Test min_version across nodes\n    let node_ids = vec![\n        \"node-0\".to_string(),\n        \"node-1\".to_string(),\n        \"node-2\".to_string(),\n    ];\n    let min_version = broadcast.min_node_version(index, &node_ids).await;\n    assert_eq!(min_version, Some(1));  // minimum is 1 (node-1 is stale)\n\n    // Test that stale node is filtered out when using version floor\n    // First, collect versions for all nodes\n    let mut node_versions = Vec::new();\n    for node_id in &node_ids {\n        let version = broadcast.node_version(index, node_id).await;\n        node_versions.push((node_id.clone(), version));\n    }\n\n    let eligible_nodes: Vec<_> = node_versions\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|(_, version)| *version >= 2)\n        .map(|(node_id, _)| node_id)\n        .collect();\n\n    assert_eq!(eligible_nodes.len(), 2);  // Only node-0 and node-2\n    assert!(!eligible_nodes.contains(&&\"node-1\".to_string()));  // node-1 excluded\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 5: Legacy sequential strategy still works\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_5_legacy_sequential_strategy_compatibility() {\n    // Verify that the two-phase broadcast can handle legacy sequential mode\n    // This test verifies the SettingsBroadcast struct itself doesn't block\n    // sequential mode (sequential mode is implemented at the proxy level)\n\n    let task_store = Arc::new(MockTaskStore::new());\n    let broadcast = SettingsBroadcast::with_task_store(task_store);\n    let index = \"products\";\n\n    // In sequential mode, the proxy would call the legacy update_settings_broadcast_legacy\n    // function, which doesn't use SettingsBroadcast at all.\n\n    // Verify that SettingsBroadcast doesn't interfere with sequential operations\n    // by checking that we can start/complete broadcasts independently\n\n    // Start a broadcast\n    let settings = json!({\"rankingRules\": [\"words\"]});\n    broadcast\n sequential mode not using broadcast coordinator\n    broadcast\n        .abort(index, \"sequential mode bypass\".to_string())\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify it's no longer [\"words\", \"typo\", \"proximity\"]\n    });\n\n    // Different key order should produce same fingerprint\n    let fp1 = fingerprint_settings(&settings1);\n    let fp2 = fingerprint_settings(&settings2);\n    assert_eq!(fp1, fp2, \"fingerprint should be order-independent\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Mode C work-queued chunked jobs coordinator (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n//!\n//! Any pod can claim a queued job via compare-and-swap. Jobs have claim TTL\n//! with heartbeats; expired claims are released for reclamation.\n//!\n//! Large jobs are split into chunks on input boundaries by the first pod\n//! that picks them up. Each chunk is an independent job with a parent reference.\n//!\n//! Applied to:\n//! - §13.9 streaming dump import — chunks on NDJSON line boundaries\n//! - §13.1 reshard backfill — partitions by shard-id range\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::task_store::{JobRow, NewJob, TaskStore};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\n\n/// Job states (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum JobState {\n    /// Job is queued and waiting to be claimed.\n    Queued = 0,\n    /// Job is claimed and in progress.\n    InProgress = 1,\n    /// Job completed successfully.\n    Completed = 2,\n    /// Job failed.\n    Failed = 3,\n}\n\nimpl JobState {\n    /// Parse from string.\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        match s {\n            \"queued\" => Some(Self::Queued),\n            \"in_progress\" => Some(Self::InProgress),\n            \"completed\" => Some(Self::Completed),\n            \"failed\" => Some(Self::Failed),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Convert to string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Queued => \"queued\",\n            Self::InProgress => \"in_progress\",\n            Self::Completed => \"completed\",\n            Self::Failed => \"failed\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Job types supported by Mode C coordinator.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum JobType {\n    /// Streaming dump import (plan §13.9).\n    DumpImport = 0,\n    /// Reshard backfill (plan §13.1).\n    ReshardBackfill = 1,\n}\n\nimpl JobType {\n    /// Parse from string.\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        match s {\n            \"dump_import\" => Some(Self::DumpImport),\n            \"reshard_backfill\" => Some(Self::ReshardBackfill),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Convert to string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::DumpImport => \"dump_import\",\n            Self::ReshardBackfill => \"reshard_backfill\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Job progress tracking.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobProgress {\n    /// Bytes processed so far (for dump import).\n    pub bytes_processed: u64,\n    /// Documents routed so far (for dump import).\n    pub docs_routed: u64,\n    /// Last cursor position for idempotent resume.\n    pub last_cursor: String,\n    /// Any error message.\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for JobProgress {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            bytes_processed: 0,\n            docs_routed: 0,\n            last_cursor: String::new(),\n            error: None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Chunk specification for a job.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobChunk {\n    /// Chunk index (0-based).\n    pub index: u32,\n    /// Total number of chunks.\n    pub total: u32,\n    /// Starting position (cursor or byte offset).\n    pub start: String,\n    /// Ending position (cursor or byte offset).\n    pub end: String,\n    /// Estimated size in bytes.\n    pub size_bytes: u64,\n}\n\n/// Job parameters.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobParams {\n    /// Index UID being operated on.\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    /// Primary key field (for dump import).\n    pub primary_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Shard count (for dump import routing).\n    pub shard_count: Option<u32>,\n    /// Old shard count (for reshard backfill).\n    pub old_shards: Option<u32>,\n    /// Target shard count (for reshard backfill).\n    pub target_shards: Option<u32>,\n    /// Shadow index UID (for reshard backfill).\n    pub shadow_index: Option<String>,\n    /// Chunk specification (if this is a chunk).\n    pub chunk: Option<JobChunk>,\n    /// Source data location (for dump import).\n    pub source_url: Option<String>,\n    /// Source data size (for dump import).\n    pub source_size_bytes: Option<u64>,\n}\n\n/// Mode C job coordinator.\npub struct ModeCCoordinator {\n    /// Task store for job persistence.\n    task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    /// Pod ID for claiming jobs.\n    pod_id: String,\n    /// Claim TTL in milliseconds (default 30s).\n    claim_ttl_ms: i64,\n    /// Heartbeat interval in milliseconds (default 10s).\n    heartbeat_interval_ms: i64,\n    /// Default chunk size in bytes (default 256 MiB).\n    default_chunk_size_bytes: u64,\n}\n\nimpl ModeCCoordinator {\n    /// Create a new Mode C coordinator.\n    pub fn new(\n        task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n        pod_id: String,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            task_store,\n            pod_id,\n            claim_ttl_ms: 30_000,  // 30 seconds\n            heartbeat_interval_ms: 10_000,  // 10 seconds\n            default_chunk_size_bytes: 268_435_456,  // 256 MiB\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the claim TTL.\n    pub fn with_claim_ttl_ms(mut self, ttl_ms: i64) -> Self {\n        self.claim_ttl_ms = ttl_ms;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the heartbeat interval.\n    pub fn with_heartbeat_interval_ms(mut self, interval_ms: i64) -> Self {\n        self.heartbeat_interval_ms = interval_ms;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the default chunk size.\n    pub fn with_chunk_size_bytes(mut self, size_bytes: u64) -> Self {\n        self.default_chunk_size_bytes = size_bytes;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Enqueue a new job.\n    pub fn enqueue_job(\n        &self,\n        type_: JobType,\n        params: JobParams,\n    ) -> Result<String> {\n        let job_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", type_.as_str(), uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n        let params_json = serde_json::to_string(&params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize params: {}\", e)))?;\n        let progress = JobProgress::default();\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let new_job = NewJob {\n            id: job_id.clone(),\n            type_: type_.as_str().to_string(),\n            params: params_json,\n            state: JobState::Queued.as_str().to_string(),\n            progress: progress_json,\n            parent_job_id: None,\n            chunk_index: None,\n            total_chunks: None,\n        };\n\n        self.task_store.insert_job(&new_job)?;\n\n        debug!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            job_type = %type_.as_str(),\n            \"enqueued new Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(job_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Try to claim a queued job.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the claimed job if successful, or None if no jobs are available.\n    pub fn claim_job(&self) -> Result<Option<ClaimedJob>> {\n        // List queued jobs\n        let queued_jobs = self.task_store.list_jobs_by_state(JobState::Queued.as_str())?;\n\n        if queued_jobs.is_empty() {\n            return Ok(None);\n        }\n\n        // Try to claim the first available job\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let claim_expires_at = now + self.claim_ttl_ms;\n\n        for job in queued_jobs {\n            if self.task_store.claim_job(&job.id, &self.pod_id, claim_expires_at)? {\n                // Successfully claimed\n                debug!(\n                    job_id = %job.id,\n                    pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n                    \"claimed Mode C job\"\n                );\n\n                return Ok(Some(ClaimedJob {\n                    id: job.id.clone(),\n                    type_: job.type_.clone(),\n                    params: job.params.clone(),\n                    progress: job.progress.clone(),\n                    claimed_by: self.pod_id.clone(),\n                    claim_expires_at,\n                    parent_job_id: job.parent_job_id.clone(),\n                    chunk_index: job.chunk_index,\n                    total_chunks: job.total_chunks,\n                }));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // All queued jobs were claimed by another pod\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n\n    /// Renew a job claim (heartbeat).\n    ///\n    /// Returns true if the claim was renewed, false if we lost the claim.\n    pub fn renew_claim(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let claim_expires_at = now + self.claim_ttl_ms;\n\n        let renewed = self.task_store.renew_job_claim(job_id, claim_expires_at)?;\n\n        if !renewed {\n            warn!(\n                job_id = %job_id,\n                pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n                \"failed to renew job claim - may have lost ownership\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(renewed)\n    }\n\n    /// Update job progress.\n    pub fn update_progress(\n        &self,\n        job_id: &str,\n        progress: &JobProgress,\n        state: JobState,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        self.task_store.update_job_progress(job_id, state.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n        debug!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            state = %state.as_str(),\n            bytes_processed = progress.bytes_processed,\n            \"updated job progress\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Complete a job successfully.\n    pub fn complete_job(&self, job_id: &str, progress: &JobProgress) -> Result<()> {\n        self.update_progress(job_id, progress, JobState::Completed)?;\n\n        info!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            \"completed Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Fail a job.\n    pub fn fail_job(&self, job_id: &str, progress: &JobProgress, error: String) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut failed_progress = progress.clone();\n        failed_progress.error = Some(error.clone());\n\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&failed_progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        self.task_store.update_job_progress(job_id, JobState::Failed.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n        error!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            error = %error,\n            \"failed Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Split a large job into chunks and enqueue them.\n    ///\n    /// Called by the first pod that picks up a large job. The original job\n    /// transitions to \"delegated\" state and child chunk jobs are created.\n    pub fn split_job_into_chunks(\n        &self,\n        job: &ClaimedJob,\n        chunk_specs: Vec<JobChunk>,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n        let params: JobParams = serde_json::from_str(&job.params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize params: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let total_chunks = chunk_specs.len() as u32;\n        let mut chunk_job_ids = Vec::new();\n\n        // Mark the parent job as delegated (in_progress with special progress)\n        let delegated_progress = JobProgress {\n            bytes_processed: 0,\n            docs_routed: 0,\n            last_cursor: \"delegated\".to_string(),\n            error: None,\n        };\n        self.update_progress(&job.id, &delegated_progress, JobState::InProgress)?;\n\n        // Create chunk jobs\n        for (idx, chunk) in chunk_specs.iter().enumerate() {\n            let mut chunk_params = params.clone();\n            chunk_params.chunk = Some(chunk.clone());\n\n            let chunk_job_id = format!(\"{}-chunk-{}\", job.id, idx);\n            let params_json = serde_json::to_string(&chunk_params)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize chunk params: {}\", e)))?;\n            let progress = JobProgress::default();\n            let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            let new_job = NewJob {\n                id: chunk_job_id.clone(),\n                type_: job.type_.clone(),\n                params: params_json,\n                state: JobState::Queued.as_str().to_string(),\n                progress: progress_json,\n                parent_job_id: Some(job.id.clone()),\n                chunk_index: Some(idx as i64),\n                total_chunks: Some(total_chunks as i64),\n            };\n\n            self.task_store.insert_job(&new_job)?;\n            chunk_job_ids.push(chunk_job_id);\n        }\n\n        info!(\n            parent_job_id = %job.id,\n            chunk_count = total_chunks,\n            \"split job into chunks\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(chunk_job_ids)\n    }\n\n    /// Reclaim expired claims.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the number of claims reclaimed.\n    pub fn reclaim_expired_claims(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let expired_jobs = self.task_store.list_expired_claims(now)?;\n\n        let mut reclaimed = 0;\n        for job in expired_jobs {\n            // Reset the job to queued state\n            let progress = JobProgress::default();\n            let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            // Clear claim and reset to queued\n            // Note: We need to update the job to clear claimed_by and claim_expires_at\n            // This is done via update_job_progress which also changes state to queued\n            self.task_store.update_job_progress(&job.id, JobState::Queued.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n            debug!(\n                job_id = %job.id,\n                previous_claimant = ?job.claimed_by,\n                \"reclaimed expired job claim\"\n            );\n\n            reclaimed += 1;\n        }\n\n        if reclaimed > 0 {\n            info!(\n                count = reclaimed,\n                \"reclaimed expired job claims\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(reclaimed)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the queue depth (number of queued jobs).\n    ///\n    /// Used for HPA scaling per plan §14.4.\n    pub fn queue_depth(&self) -> Result<u64> {\n        self.task_store.count_jobs_by_state(JobState::Queued.as_str())\n    }\n\n    /// Get job by ID.\n    pub fn get_job(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<Option<JobRow>> {\n        self.task_store.get_job(job_id)\n    }\n\n    /// List all chunks for a parent job.\n    pub fn list_chunks(&self, parent_job_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<JobRow>> {\n        self.task_store.list_jobs_by_parent(parent_job_id)\n    }\n}\n\n/// A claimed job being processed by a pod.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClaimedJob {\n    /// Job ID.\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Job type.\n    pub type_: String,\n    /// Job parameters (JSON).\n    pub params: String,\n    /// Job progress (JSON).\n    pub progress: String,\n    /// Pod that claimed this job.\n    pub claimed_by: String,\n    /// When the claim expires (UNIX ms).\n    pub claim_expires_at: i64,\n    /// Parent job ID if this is a chunk.\n    pub parent_job_id: Option<String>,\n    /// Chunk index if this is a chunk.\n    pub chunk_index: Option<i64>,\n    /// Total chunks if this is part of a chunked job.\n    pub total_chunks: Option<i64>,\n}\n\nimpl ClaimedJob {\n    /// Parse the job parameters.\n    pub fn parse_params(&self) -> Result<JobParams> {\n        serde_json::from_str(&self.params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize params: {}\", e)))\n    }\n\n    /// Parse the current progress.\n    pub fn parse_progress(&self) -> Result<JobProgress> {\n        serde_json::from_str(&self.progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize progress: {}\", e)))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a chunk job.\n    pub fn is_chunk(&self) -> bool {\n        self.parent_job_id.is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the claim is about to expire (within 5 seconds).\n    pub fn claim_expiring_soon(&self) -> bool {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        self.claim_expires_at - now < 5_000\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as i64\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore;\n\n    fn test_coordinator() -> ModeCCoordinator {\n        let store = Arc::new(SqliteTaskStore::open_in_memory().unwrap());\n        store.migrate().unwrap();\n        ModeCCoordinator::new(store, \"test-pod\".to_string())\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_job_state_roundtrip() {\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"queued\"), Some(JobState::Queued));\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"in_progress\"), Some(JobState::InProgress));\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"completed\"), Some(JobState::Completed));\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"failed\"), Some(JobState::Failed));\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"unknown\"), None);\n\n        assert_eq!(JobState::Queued.as_str(), \"queued\");\n        assert_eq!(JobState::InProgress.as_str(), \"in_progress\");\n        assert_eq!(JobState::Completed.as_str(), \"completed\");\n        assert_eq!(JobState::Failed.as_str(), \"failed\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_job_type_roundtrip() {\n        assert_eq!(JobType::from_str(\"dump_import\"), Some(JobType::DumpImport));\n        assert_eq!(JobType::from_str(\"reshard_backfill\"), Some(JobType::ReshardBackfill));\n        assert_eq!(JobType::from_str(\"unknown\"), None);\n\n        assert_eq!(JobType::DumpImport.as_str(), \"dump_import\");\n        assert_eq!(JobType::ReshardBackfill.as_str(), \"reshard_backfill\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_enqueue_and_claim_job() {\n        let coord = test_coordinator();\n\n        let params = JobParams {\n            index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n            primary_key: Some(\"id\".to_string()),\n            shard_count: Some(64),\n            old_shards: None,\n            target_shards: None,\n            shadow_index: None,\n            chunk: None,\n            source_url: Some(\"https://example.com/dump.ndjson\".to_string()),\n            source_size_bytes: Some(1_000_000_000),\n        };\n\n        let job_id = coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim the job\n        let claimed = coord.claim_job().unwrap();\n        assert!(claimed.is_some());\n        let claimed = claimed.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claimed.id, job_id);\n        assert_eq!(claimed.claimed_by, \"test-pod\");\n\n        // Parse params\n        let parsed_params = claimed.parse_params().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(parsed_params.index_uid, \"test-index\");\n        assert_eq!(parsed_params.primary_key, Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_renewal() {\n        let coord = test_coordinator();\n\n        let params = JobParams {\n            index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n            primary_key: Mode C work-queued chunked jobs coordinator (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n//!\n//! Any pod can claim a queued job via compare-and-swap. Jobs have claim TTL\n//! with heartbeats; expired claims are released for reclamation.\n//!\n//! Large jobs are split into chunks on input boundaries by the first pod\n//! that picks them up. Each chunk is an independent job with a parent reference.\n//!\n//! Applied to:\n//! - §13.9 streaming dump import — chunks on NDJSON line boundaries\n//! - §13.1 reshard backfill — partitions by shard-id range\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::task_store::{JobRow, NewJob, TaskStore};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\n\n/// Job states (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum JobState {\n    /// Job is queued and waiting to be claimed.\n    Queued = 0,\n    /// Job is claimed and in progress.\n    InProgress = 1,\n    /// Job completed successfully.\n    Completed = 2,\n    /// Job failed.\n    Failed = 3,\n}\n\nimpl JobState {\n    /// Parse from string.\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        match s {\n            \"queued\" => Some(Self::Queued),\n            \"in_progress\" => Some(Self::InProgress),\n            \"completed\" => Some(Self::Completed),\n            \"failed\" => Some(Self::Failed),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Convert to string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Queued => \"queued\",\n            Self::InProgress => \"in_progress\",\n            Self::Completed => \"completed\",\n            Self::Failed => \"failed\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Job types supported by Mode C coordinator.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum JobType {\n    /// Streaming dump import (plan §13.9).\n    DumpImport = 0,\n    /// Reshard backfill (plan §13.1).\n    ReshardBackfill = 1,\n}\n\nimpl JobType {\n    /// Parse from string.\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        match s {\n            \"dump_import\" => Some(Self::DumpImport),\n            \"reshard_backfill\" => Some(Self::ReshardBackfill),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Convert to string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::DumpImport => \"dump_import\",\n            Self::ReshardBackfill => \"reshard_backfill\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Job progress tracking.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobProgress {\n    /// Bytes processed so far (for dump import).\n    pub bytes_processed: u64,\n    /// Documents routed so far (for dump import).\n    pub docs_routed: u64,\n    /// Last cursor position for idempotent resume.\n    pub last_cursor: String,\n    /// Any error message.\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for JobProgress {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            bytes_processed: 0,\n            docs_routed: 0,\n            last_cursor: String::new(),\n            error: None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Chunk specification for a job.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobChunk {\n    /// Chunk index (0-based).\n    pub index: u32,\n    /// Total number of chunks.\n    pub total: u32,\n    /// Starting position (cursor or byte offset).\n    pub start: String,\n    /// Ending position (cursor or byte offset).\n    pub end: String,\n    /// Estimated size in bytes.\n    pub size_bytes: u64,\n}\n\n/// Job parameters.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobParams {\n    /// Index UID being operated on.\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    /// Primary key field (for dump import).\n    pub primary_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Shard count (for dump import routing).\n    pub shard_count: Option<u32>,\n    /// Old shard count (for reshard backfill).\n    pub old_shards: Option<u32>,\n    /// Target shard count (for reshard backfill).\n    pub target_shards: Option<u32>,\n    /// Shadow index UID (for reshard backfill).\n    pub shadow_index: Option<String>,\n    /// Chunk specification (if this is a chunk).\n    pub chunk: Option<JobChunk>,\n    /// Source data location (for dump import).\n    pub source_url: Option<String>,\n    /// Source data size (for dump import).\n    pub source_size_bytes: Option<u64>,\n}\n\n/// Mode C job coordinator.\npub struct ModeCCoordinator {\n    /// Task store for job persistence.\n    task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    /// Pod ID for claiming jobs.\n    pod_id: String,\n    /// Claim TTL in milliseconds (default 30s).\n    claim_ttl_ms: i64,\n    /// Heartbeat interval in milliseconds (default 10s).\n    heartbeat_interval_ms: i64,\n    /// Default chunk size in bytes (default 256 MiB).\n    default_chunk_size_bytes: u64,\n}\n\nimpl ModeCCoordinator {\n    /// Create a new Mode C coordinator.\n    pub fn new(\n        task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n        pod_id: String,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            task_store,\n            pod_id,\n            claim_ttl_ms: 30_000,  // 30 seconds\n            heartbeat_interval_ms: 10_000,  // 10 seconds\n            default_chunk_size_bytes: 268_435_456,  // 256 MiB\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the claim TTL.\n    pub fn with_claim_ttl_ms(mut self, ttl_ms: i64) -> Self {\n        self.claim_ttl_ms = ttl_ms;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the heartbeat interval.\n    pub fn with_heartbeat_interval_ms(mut self, interval_ms: i64) -> Self {\n        self.heartbeat_interval_ms = interval_ms;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the default chunk size.\n    pub fn with_chunk_size_bytes(mut self, size_bytes: u64) -> Self {\n        self.default_chunk_size_bytes = size_bytes;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Enqueue a new job.\n    pub fn enqueue_job(\n        &self,\n        type_: JobType,\n        params: JobParams,\n    ) -> Result<String> {\n        let job_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", type_.as_str(), uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n        let params_json = serde_json::to_string(&params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize params: {}\", e)))?;\n        let progress = JobProgress::default();\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let new_job = NewJob {\n            id: job_id.clone(),\n            type_: type_.as_str().to_string(),\n            params: params_json,\n            state: JobState::Queued.as_str().to_string(),\n            progress: progress_json,\n            parent_job_id: None,\n            chunk_index: None,\n            total_chunks: None,\n            created_at: millis_now(),\n        };\n\n        self.task_store.insert_job(&new_job)?;\n\n        debug!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            job_type = %type_.as_str(),\n            \"enqueued new Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(job_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Try to claim a queued job.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the claimed job if successful, or None if no jobs are available.\n    pub fn claim_job(&self) -> Result<Option<ClaimedJob>> {\n        // List queued jobs\n        let queued_jobs = self.task_store.list_jobs_by_state(JobState::Queued.as_str())?;\n\n        if queued_jobs.is_empty() {\n            return Ok(None);\n        }\n\n        // Try to claim the first available job\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let claim_expires_at = now + self.claim_ttl_ms;\n\n        for job in queued_jobs {\n            if self.task_store.claim_job(&job.id, &self.pod_id, claim_expires_at)? {\n                // Successfully claimed\n                debug!(\n                    job_id = %job.id,\n                    pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n                    \"claimed Mode C job\"\n                );\n\n                return Ok(Some(ClaimedJob {\n                    id: job.id.clone(),\n                    type_: job.type_.clone(),\n                    params: job.params.clone(),\n                    progress: job.progress.clone(),\n                    claimed_by: self.pod_id.clone(),\n                    claim_expires_at,\n                    parent_job_id: job.parent_job_id.clone(),\n                    chunk_index: job.chunk_index,\n                    total_chunks: job.total_chunks,\n                }));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // All queued jobs were claimed by another pod\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n\n    /// Renew a job claim (heartbeat).\n    ///\n    /// Returns true if the claim was renewed, false if we lost the claim.\n    pub fn renew_claim(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let claim_expires_at = now + self.claim_ttl_ms;\n\n        let renewed = self.task_store.renew_job_claim(job_id, claim_expires_at)?;\n\n        if !renewed {\n            warn!(\n                job_id = %job_id,\n                pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n                \"failed to renew job claim - may have lost ownership\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(renewed)\n    }\n\n    /// Update job progress.\n    pub fn update_progress(\n        &self,\n        job_id: &str,\n        progress: &JobProgress,\n        state: JobState,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        self.task_store.update_job_progress(job_id, state.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n        debug!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            state = %state.as_str(),\n            bytes_processed = progress.bytes_processed,\n            \"updated job progress\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Complete a job successfully.\n    pub fn complete_job(&self, job_id: &str, progress: &JobProgress) -> Result<()> {\n        self.update_progress(job_id, progress, JobState::Completed)?;\n\n        info!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            \"completed Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Fail a job.\n    pub fn fail_job(&self, job_id: &str, progress: &JobProgress, error: String) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut failed_progress = progress.clone();\n        failed_progress.error = Some(error.clone());\n\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&failed_progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        self.task_store.update_job_progress(job_id, JobState::Failed.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n        error!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            error = %error,\n            \"failed Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Split a large job into chunks and enqueue them.\n    ///\n    /// Called by the first pod that picks up a large job. The original job\n    /// transitions to \"delegated\" state and child chunk jobs are created.\n    pub fn split_job_into_chunks(\n        &self,\n        job: &ClaimedJob,\n        chunk_specs: Vec<JobChunk>,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n        let params: JobParams = serde_json::from_str(&job.params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize params: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let total_chunks = chunk_specs.len() as u32;\n        let mut chunk_job_ids = Vec::new();\n\n        // Mark the parent job as delegated (in_progress with special progress)\n        let delegated_progress = JobProgress {\n            bytes_processed: 0,\n            docs_routed: 0,\n            last_cursor: \"delegated\".to_string(),\n            error: None,\n        };\n        self.update_progress(&job.id, &delegated_progress, JobState::InProgress)?;\n\n        // Create chunk jobs\n        for (idx, chunk) in chunk_specs.iter().enumerate() {\n            let mut chunk_params = params.clone();\n            chunk_params.chunk = Some(chunk.clone());\n\n            let chunk_job_id = format!(\"{}-chunk-{}\", job.id, idx);\n            let params_json = serde_json::to_string(&chunk_params)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize chunk params: {}\", e)))?;\n            let progress = JobProgress::default();\n            let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            let new_job = NewJob {\n                id: chunk_job_id.clone(),\n                type_: job.type_.clone(),\n                params: params_json,\n                state: JobState::Queued.as_str().to_string(),\n                progress: progress_json,\n                parent_job_id: Some(job.id.clone()),\n                chunk_index: Some(idx as i64),\n                total_chunks: Some(total_chunks as i64),\n            };\n\n            self.task_store.insert_job(&new_job)?;\n            chunk_job_ids.push(chunk_job_id);\n        }\n\n        info!(\n            parent_job_id = %job.id,\n            chunk_count = total_chunks,\n            \"split job into chunks\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(chunk_job_ids)\n    }\n\n    /// Reclaim expired claims.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the number of claims reclaimed.\n    pub fn reclaim_expired_claims(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let expired_jobs = self.task_store.list_expired_claims(now)?;\n\n        let mut reclaimed = 0;\n        for job in expired_jobs {\n            // Reset the job to queued state\n            let progress = JobProgress::default();\n            let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            // Clear claim and reset to queued\n            // Note: We need to update the job to clear claimed_by and claim_expires_at\n            // This is done via update_job_progress which also changes state to queued\n            self.task_store.update_job_progress(&job.id, JobState::Queued.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n            debug!(\n                job_id = %job.id,\n                previous_claimant = ?job.claimed_by,\n                \"reclaimed expired job claim\"\n            );\n\n            reclaimed += 1;\n        }\n\n        if reclaimed > 0 {\n            info!(\n                count = reclaimed,\n                \"reclaimed expired job claims\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(reclaimed)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the queue depth (number of queued jobs).\n    ///\n    /// Used for HPA scaling per plan §14.4.\n    pub fn queue_depth(&self) -> Result<u64> {\n        self.task_store.count_jobs_by_state(JobState::Queued.as_str())\n    }\n\n    /// Get job by ID.\n    pub fn get_job(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<Option<JobRow>> {\n        self.task_store.get_job(job_id)\n    }\n\n    /// List all chunks for a parent job.\n    pub fn list_chunks(&self, parent_job_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<JobRow>> {\n        self.task_store.list_jobs_by_parent(parent_job_id)\n    }\n}\n\n/// A claimed job being processed by a pod.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClaimedJob {\n    /// Job ID.\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Job type.\n    pub type_: String,\n    /// Job parameters (JSON).\n    pub params: String,\n    /// Job progress (JSON).\n    pub progress: String,\n    /// Pod that claimed this job.\n    pub claimed_by: String,\n    /// When the claim expires (UNIX ms).\n    pub claim_expires_at: i64,\n    /// Parent job ID if this is a chunk.\n    pub parent_job_id: Option<String>,\n    /// Chunk index if this is a chunk.\n    pub chunk_index: Option<i64>,\n    /// Total chunks if this is part of a chunked job.\n    pub total_chunks: Option<i64>,\n}\n\nimpl ClaimedJob {\n    /// Parse the job parameters.\n    pub fn parse_params(&self) -> Result<JobParams> {\n        serde_json::from_str(&self.params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize params: {}\", e)))\n    }\n\n    /// Parse the current progress.\n    pub fn parse_progress(&self) -> Result<JobProgress> {\n        serde_json::from_str(&self.progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize progress: {}\", e)))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a chunk job.\n    pub fn is_chunk(&self) -> bool {\n        self.parent_job_id.is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the claim is about to expire (within 5 seconds).\n    pub fn claim_expiring_soon(&self) -> bool {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        self.claim_expires_at - now < 5_000\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as i64\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore;\n\n    fn
